title: Lemma 'geometric slices': undefined notion + asserted key equivalence

comments.author: human
comments.body:
In Lemma 2 (geometric slices), the term **'geometric Mackey functor $M$'** is never defined, yet the entire argument rests on it. The proof asserts that for any finite $G$-set $T$, the fixed-point inclusion $S^{|T/G|}\hookrightarrow N^T S^1$ induces an **equivalence** $S^{|T/G|}\otimes HM \xrightarrow{\sim} N^T S^1\otimes HM$. This is the crux of the converse and is stated without proof.

Since equivalences in $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ are detected by $\Phi^K$ for **all** $K\subseteq G$, one must check the equivalence on $\Phi^K$ for every $K$, not merely $K=G$. The proof only invokes $\Phi^G$. Moreover the bracket computation
$$[N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G\cong[\Phi^GN^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM(G/G)]\cong[S^{|T/G|},\Sigma^kHM(G/G)]$$
is itself nontrivial: it requires $HM$ to be concentrated over $G/G$ (precisely the missing 'geometric' hypothesis) and the identification of $\Phi^G$-mapping with genuine mapping. None of this is justified.

**To close:** (i) define 'geometric Mackey functor' character-by-character; (ii) prove the equivalence by computing $\Phi^K(N^TS^1\otimes HM)$ for all $K$; (iii) state the bracket identity as a lemma with verified hypotheses.
